original_text: A nuclear disarmament treaty is signed by the United States, Russia, and China.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A non-binding joint statement expressing intent to explore future nuclear risk reduction talks is issued by the United States, Russia, and China.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A limited agreement freezing the production of new nuclear warheads, without requiring any reductions to existing stockpiles, is signed by the United States, Russia, and China.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A treaty mandating modest reductions in deployed strategic nuclear warheads, while leaving tactical weapons and reserve stockpiles untouched, is signed by the United States, Russia, and China.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A nuclear disarmament treaty is signed by the United States, Russia, and China.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A nuclear disarmament treaty with binding timelines, rigorous on-site verification mechanisms, and deep reductions across all categories of nuclear weapons is signed by the United States, Russia, and China.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A comprehensive nuclear disarmament treaty requiring the near-total elimination and irreversible dismantlement of all nuclear warheads, delivery systems, and production infrastructure, with intrusive international verification, is signed by the United States, Russia, and China.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A comprehensive and irreversible treaty achieving the complete elimination of all nuclear weapons, warheads, delivery systems, and production capabilities worldwide, with permanent international verification and enforcement, is signed by the United States, Russia, and China.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the depth and scope of disarmament commitments: from a non-binding statement (tier 1), to a production freeze (tier 2), to partial reductions (tier 3), to a general disarmament treaty (tier 4), to a verified deep-cuts treaty (tier 5), to near-total elimination (tier 6), to complete global elimination with enforcement (tier 7). The core scenario—a nuclear disarmament agreement among major powers—is preserved throughout.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because the complete, verified, and irreversible global elimination of all nuclear weapons maximizes security and minimizes existential risk, representing the fullest possible realization of nuclear disarmament.
